tasks.title: Added 41 tests for admin menu CRUD API routes and hidden-item filtering, bringing total from 111 to 152
tasks.one_liner: Added 41 tests for admin menu CRUD API routes and hidden-item filtering, bringing total from 111 to 152
tasks.narrative:
Created two comprehensive test files covering all admin menu API routes and hidden-item filtering behavior.

**menu-crud.test.ts** (31 tests) — Tests all 5 HTTP methods on the admin menu API by mocking `@/lib/prisma` and calling route handlers directly with mock Request objects:
- GET: returns all items including hidden ones, handles DB errors (500)
- POST: creates with valid data (201), validates missing name (400), empty name (400), price ≤ 0 (400), negative price (400), non-integer price (400), invalid category (400), invalid JSON (400), trims whitespace, sets defaults, accepts optional description
- PUT: updates fields (200), 404 for non-existent ID, 400 for NaN ID, validates empty name, price ≤ 0, invalid category, invalid JSON, non-integer sortOrder, non-boolean available
- PATCH: toggles available flag, toggles hidden flag, 404 for non-existent, 400 for invalid field name, 400 for non-boolean value, 400 for invalid ID
- DELETE: soft-deletes by setting hidden=true, 404 for non-existent, 400 for invalid ID

**hidden-menu-filter.test.ts** (10 tests) — Tests hidden-item filtering across customer, staff, and order surfaces:
- Staff menu route uses `{ hidden: false }` filter
- Empty result when all items hidden
- Contract test confirming staff route uses same filter shape as customer page
- Order creation rejects hidden items with 400 (not 409)
- Hidden check runs before unavailable check (precedence)
- Unavailable (non-hidden) returns 409
- Visible+available items create order successfully (201)
- Mixed hidden+visible order rejects entire order
- Missing table returns 404
- Non-existent menu item returns 400

Both test files follow the project's existing pattern: vi.mock for Prisma, direct route handler invocation, and assertion on response status codes and JSON bodies.

tasks.description:
Refactor GET /api/admin/qr-pdf to source tables from the DB instead of TABLE_COUNT env var. The route currently iterates 1..TABLE_COUNT generating QR codes sequentially. Change it to call `prisma.table.findMany({ orderBy: { number: 'asc' } })` and generate one QR per DB table record. Each QR encodes `http://${shopIp}:${shopPort}/order?table=${table.number}` (using table.number, not id). Add `import { prisma } from '@/lib/prisma'`. If zero tables exist, return 400 with error message 'Chưa có bàn nào. Hãy thêm bàn trong trang quản lý.' Keep all auth, font validation, and PDF layout logic intact. Add tests `src/app/api/admin/qr-pdf/__tests__/qr-pdf.test.ts` covering: 1) unauthenticated → 401, 2) SHOP_IP missing → 400, 3) no tables in DB → 400, 4) 1 table → PDF with 1 QR, 5) multiple tables → PDF with correct count. Use `import { setupServer } from 'msw/node'` with msw handlers mocking prisma.table.findMany. Use `PDFDocument` to parse the generated PDF and verify it has correct page count and image count.

**Files:** src/app/api/admin/qr-pdf/route.ts, src/app/api/admin/qr-pdf/__tests__/qr-pdf.test.ts, src/lib/prisma.ts

**Verification:** vitest run -- src/app/api/admin/qr-pdf/__tests__/qr-pdf.test.ts && grep -c 'prisma.table.findMany' src/app/api/admin/qr-pdf/route.ts

decisions.decision: Tech stack cho hệ thống quản lý quán trà sữa
decisions.choice: Next.js 14+ (App Router) + Tailwind CSS + SQLite (Prisma) + SSE
decisions.rationale: Next.js cho full-stack trong một project. SQLite không cần cài DB server riêng — phù hợp chạy local. Tailwind cho UI nhanh. SSE cho real-time đơn giản không cần WebSocket server.

decisions.choice: QR tĩnh chứa URL http://<local-ip>:3000/order?table=N, in sẵn 10-20 mã dán tại bàn
decisions.rationale: QR tĩnh đơn giản, in một lần dùng mãi. Không cần dynamic QR hay session — table number là identity đủ cho quán tầm trung.

decisions.decision: Ai có quyền huỷ/sửa đơn hàng
decisions.choice: Chỉ nhân viên được huỷ/sửa đơn từ dashboard. Khách không có nút huỷ/sửa trên trang order — cần báo nhân viên.
decisions.rationale: Tránh khách tự huỷ đơn gây mất kiểm soát. Quán tầm trung nhân viên gần bàn, báo trực tiếp nhanh hơn. Giữ UI khách đơn giản, logic phức tạp dồn về phía staff.

decisions.decision: PDF generation library for QR code printables
decisions.choice: pdfkit + qrcode npm packages — server-side PDF generation in Next.js Route Handler
decisions.rationale: PDFKit is pure Node.js with streaming API, supports A4 sizing and PNG image embedding from Buffer natively. Combined with qrcode's toBuffer() for PNG generation, gives a clean server-side pipeline with no browser dependencies. Alternatives rejected: jspdf (browser-first), @react-pdf/renderer (heavyweight overkill). Both qrcode and pdfkit are offline-compatible (R006).

decisions.choice: Simple ADMIN_PASSWORD env var + httpOnly cookie. Next.js middleware on Edge runtime protects /admin/* except /admin/login. No hashing — plain comparison. Cookie set with sameSite:lax, no secure flag (HTTP local network).
decisions.rationale: Tea shop on local network doesn't need complex auth. Single password from env var is appropriate for admin access on internal WiFi. httpOnly cookie prevents JS theft. No rate limiting needed since attack surface is local network only. Edge-compatible middleware avoids Node.js runtime requirements.

decisions.decision: M002 slice decomposition: two vertical slices instead of three
decisions.choice: S01: Menu Browsing (read path — QR to tabbed menu display), S02: Cart + Order Submission (write path — cart state, UI, API, confirmation). Rejected: three-slice split with standalone API slice.
decisions.rationale: A standalone API-only slice is a horizontal layer, not a vertical slice — it would only be verifiable via curl, not by a real user through the real interface. The order creation API belongs with the cart submission because they form a single demoable capability. Two slices maps to the natural boundary: the read path (S01 is independently valuable — QR codes lead to a real menu) and the write path (S02 completes the ordering flow end-to-end).

decisions.decision: Order creation uses Route Handler (POST /api/order) instead of Server Action
decisions.choice: Route Handler at /api/order — JSON request/response pattern with explicit status codes and validation
decisions.rationale: Cart submission is a JSON payload from client-side state (not a form submission). Route Handler gives clean control over validation, error responses with specific status codes (400/404/409), and structured error payloads for unavailable items. Server Actions are form-oriented and would require workarounds for JSON payloads and custom error shapes.

decisions.decision: M003 slice decomposition: station-first vertical slices
decisions.choice: S01: Bar Station End-to-End (SSE + API + UI proving the risky technology), S02: Kitchen + Overview + Cancel (extending proven patterns), S03: Notifications + Auto-Hide (additive polish). Three slices ordered by risk.
decisions.rationale: SSE is the only novel technology — building the bar station end-to-end proves it works through a real user-facing feature (not a spike). Kitchen and overview reuse the proven StationView and SSE infrastructure with minimal risk. Notifications are purely additive and can be descoped. This mirrors D006 reasoning (vertical slices over horizontal layers) but applied to the station topology.

decisions.decision: SSE subscriber registry with station-level filtering
decisions.choice: Single SSE endpoint at /api/staff/orders/stream with ?station= query param. Subscriber registry on globalThis stores controller + station metadata. Broadcasts filter by station so bar clients only receive DRINK-relevant events.
decisions.rationale: Station-level SSE filtering reduces noise per client — bar doesn't need kitchen events. A single endpoint with query param is simpler than three separate SSE endpoints. The globalThis caching pattern is already proven by the Prisma singleton in this codebase. Filtering at broadcast time (not at subscription time) keeps the registry simple while still avoiding unnecessary event delivery.

decisions.decision: Shared StationView component parameterized by station type
decisions.choice: Single StationView component accepting station prop ('bar'|'kitchen'|'all'). Each station page is a thin Server Component wrapper. Station determines: SSE filter, Vietnamese label ('Quầy Bar'/'Bếp'/'Tổng quan'), and which status transitions are available (station pages: PENDING→PREPARING→READY; overview: READY→SERVED).
decisions.rationale: All three stations share the same data shape (orders with items), same real-time mechanism (SSE), and same card layout. The only differences are filtering and available transitions. A parameterized component avoids triplicate code and ensures changes propagate consistently. This is the brownfield-bias choice — it extends the existing pattern of reusable components seen in M002's CartProvider/MenuView.

decisions.decision: PAID status handling — order-level override vs item-derived
decisions.choice: PAID is an explicit order-level override set directly by staff action. deriveOrderStatus() remains unchanged (doesn't know about PAID). Item PATCH endpoint guards against modifications on PAID orders (409 Conflict).
decisions.rationale: PAID is a business action (staff confirms payment) not derivable from item progress. Items don't have a PAID status — only orders do. Keeping deriveOrderStatus pure (item-status only) avoids coupling payment logic into the core status module. The guard on PAID orders prevents re-derivation from overwriting PAID back to SERVED.

decisions.decision: Add-item from bill: which order receives new items
decisions.choice: Add new OrderItems to the latest unpaid order for the table (not create a new order).
decisions.rationale: Simpler implementation, keeps order count manageable, and the bill view already aggregates across orders so the user won't notice the difference. Creating a new order for each staff-added item would bloat the data model unnecessarily.

decisions.decision: M004 slice decomposition: bill-payment flow then add-item
decisions.choice: S01: Bill & Payment Flow (schema + APIs + checkout page + cancel-from-bill + pay + SSE). S02: Add Items from Bill (menu picker modal + add-item API). Two slices ordered by dependency.
decisions.rationale: The bill-to-payment lifecycle is the core deliverable for R004 and must work end-to-end first. Add-item from bill (R008 extension) is purely additive — the bill flow is complete without it. Splitting the menu picker modal into S02 isolates the most complex new UI component and lets S01 be validated independently. Follows D006 precedent (read path first, write path second).

decisions.decision: M005 slice decomposition: 4 slices with schema-first ordering
decisions.choice: S01: Admin Menu Management (schema + toast + admin shell + menu CRUD + hidden filtering), S02: Table Management + QR Refactor (depends S01), S03: Skeleton Loaders + Error UX Polish (depends S01), S04: Deployment Readiness (depends S01,S02,S03). S02 and S03 can run in parallel after S01.
decisions.rationale: Schema migration (hidden field) unblocks all menu-related work and must be verified against 111 existing tests first — this is the highest-risk work. Toast component is needed by every CRUD operation across all slices, so it ships with S01 as shared infrastructure alongside the admin dashboard shell. Menu CRUD is the most complex and highest-value capability. Table management and UX polish are independent tracks that can parallelize after S01 establishes the admin foundation. Deployment readiness is pure additive and comes last.

decisions.decision: Admin dashboard: tabbed navigation at /admin with redirect to /admin/menu
decisions.choice: Replace current QR-only /admin/page.tsx with a redirect to /admin/menu. Admin layout uses bottom tab navigation (AdminNav) replicating StaffNav pattern. Three tabs: Thực đơn (/admin/menu), Bàn (/admin/tables), QR Code (/admin/qr). Logout link in nav.
decisions.rationale: Staff dashboard already established the tabbed bottom nav pattern that works on tablets. Replicating it for admin maintains UX consistency. Three tabs is right-sized for the admin scope. Redirect from /admin to /admin/menu gives the most useful default landing page. The existing /admin auth middleware covers all new routes automatically.

decisions.decision: Toast notification: portal-based component at root layout, z-index above all overlays
decisions.choice: ToastProvider wraps root layout with context exposing useToast() hook. Toast renders via portal at z-60+ to clear CartSheet (z-50) and modals. Green for success, red for error, auto-dismiss after 3s. No dependencies — pure React + Tailwind. Shared across admin, staff, and customer pages.
decisions.rationale: Portal-based rendering avoids z-index conflicts with existing overlays (CartSheet, MenuPickerModal). Root-level provider ensures toast is available in all page contexts without per-page setup. No toast library needed — the component is simple enough to build with existing tools. 3s auto-dismiss matches industry standard for non-critical feedback.
